cb65425b-cc3d-4f8a-8164-5849f594d708.title: Build data by onboarding 5 additional customers
cb65425b-cc3d-4f8a-8164-5849f594d708.exec_summary: Goal: onboard the next 5 hand-picked customers and run AP & AR reconciliation on neno by early July. Open Banking ingestion is the active build (Dima); per-client Exact connections are planned (Ihor). The guided onboarding home and the WhatsApp/email/Vault channels are queued next, then final QA. The main worry is timing: the onboarding-actions project is still in backlog against a 6 Jul target, and the open-banking provider decision is unconfirmed.
whats_changed: Open Banking provider-agnostic ingestion (NEO-1291) and frontend rework (NEO-1115) are in progress (Dima).

Onboarding checklist scope locked: 6 hardcoded steps (connect Swan, connect external bank, view transactions, reconcile/respond to tasks, send first invoice, contact accountant); team invites, accounting menu, WhatsApp and credit-card steps deferred.
Exact matching (AR & AP) is complete; GL-account picker (David), VAT bracket, vendor selection and refunds still outstanding.
WhatsApp/email/Vault pipeline exists; the upload drawer is still missing WhatsApp + email options (flagged 22 Jun).

risks.text: 6 Jul onboarding target is tight — the onboarding-actions project is still in backlog and its stepper depends on the new transactions UI.
Open-banking provider decision unconfirmed (Plaid ~€2k/mo minimum); single- vs multi-provider still pending.
Email forwarding only accepts workspace-member senders, has no UI yet, and the rejected-email auto-reply is still to be built.

projects.name: Connect external bank accounts (Open Banking)
projects.summary: Provider-agnostic transaction/balance ingestion plus a multi-account Money Management UI rework.

projects.name: Multi-Client Exact Online Connections
projects.summary: A separate Exact connection per client workspace so accountants reconcile each client into their own division.
blockers: GL picker, VAT bracket, vendor selection and refunds still outstanding

projects.name: Present onboarding actions for the next 5 customers
projects.summary: Home dashboard with guided steps teaching the three bill-submission channels and routing to bank connect.
blockers: Depends on the new transactions UI

projects.name: WhatsApp, Email forwarding & Vault re-provisioning
projects.summary: Make the three bill-upload channels work for customers without a Swan account.

decisions.text: Onboarding is a simple hardcoded checklist — a backend boolean/timestamp per step that hides when complete.
Onboarding fixed to 6 steps; team invites, accounting menu, WhatsApp and credit-card steps deferred.
Non-Swan customers must be able to connect to neno (bypassing the Swan requirement) — a prerequisite for the next 5.
Slack notifications required for all document uploads during the early phase.

open_questions.text: Onboarding stepper is a placeholder until the new transactions UI is ready.
Open-banking provider decision still unconfirmed — needs a status check.
Receipt upload drawer is missing WhatsApp + email options (flagged 22 Jun).

next_steps.text: Design the user-journey screens + an Ocean Ionics-specific homepage.
WhatsApp messaging update + email reply automation.
Check Swan drawer configurability; leave Linear tickets for Joel.
All team members test WhatsApp + email forwarding before Ocean Ionics rollout.

fef38f90-b82c-4c47-b377-6dcb9b1a3020.title: Enable accountants to complete VAT submissions via double-entry GL
fef38f90-b82c-4c47-b377-6dcb9b1a3020.exec_summary: Standing up neno's own double-entry general ledger so we can file Q3 VAT for ~6 customers directly from neno. The spike that writes into the previously-unwired ledger is in progress (Mark), and after the 23 Jun design session with DP the core mechanics are now decided — VAT corrections post to the next period, closed periods lock with approval-gated inserts only, and FX is handled via month-end revaluation. Next is productionising GL writes for Ocean Ionics from ~1 July. The biggest risk remains the ~13k-line, agent-generated spike, which needs careful review for atomicity and correctness before any of it ships.
whats_changed: Design session with DP (23 Jun) locked the core ledger mechanics: VAT corrections, period locking and multi-currency handling.
Spike (NEO-1302) writing into the neno GL for Ocean Ionics is in progress (Mark).
Q3 target confirmed: VAT submission from neno for 6 customers (AR & AP), opening balance taken from the Q1/Q2 Exact close.

risks.text: The spike (~13k lines, Claude-generated) took liberties with DB writes — atomicity and no overlapping bookings must be guaranteed before productionising. Review under way this week (Mark/Matthew).
Belgium gapless-ledger requirement still unconfirmed — does it constrain the day-to-day architecture or only closed-period reporting?
DP's full reporting requirements list (potentially 100+ items) is not yet compiled — could expand GL scope.

projects.name: Start writing to neno's double-entry GL
projects.summary: Wire up the unused ledger and write entries for Ocean Ionics; target real GL writes from 1 July.
blockers: Spike code needs atomicity/correctness review before production

decisions.text: VAT corrections after a period close are booked in the next period (dated day 1); amounts under €1,000 fold into the next return without a supplemental filing. Balance sheet needs three VAT lines: receivable, payable, and a corrections/suspense account.

Closed periods lock writes by default; unlocking is approval-gated and only for material post-close accruals. No editing existing entries — only inserting new journal entries dated in the closed period, with a full audit log.

Multi-currency: store foreign amount + transaction-date rate, derive euro on demand; revalue FX assets/liabilities at month-end to an unrealized FX gains/losses account (P&L under Dutch GAAP, equity under IFRS).
Insert-only architecture with reversals — no direct edits to journal entries; corrections reverse and rebook.
decisions.source: Granola: Next Steps AGL with Mark

decisions.text: Period closes define immutability; monitoring over rollback; Euro-only for now; Q3 VAT for 6 customers with opening balance from the Q1/Q2 Exact close.
decisions.source: Granola: Next Steps AGL with Mark

open_questions.text: Belgium gapless-ledger requirement — does it constrain day-to-day ledger architecture or only closed-period exports/reporting? (not resolved in the session)
Full reporting requirements list being compiled by DP (potentially 100+ items) — will frame future design.
What must change in the current system to use double-entry bookkeeping for the Q3 VAT submission?

next_steps.text: Compile the full reporting requirements list.
Compile outstanding open questions before the follow-up.
Review spike code for atomicity, security and correctness.
Reproduce the balance check in production with periods.
Map the VAT export to Nextens for submission.

meetings.title: Double-Entry Bookkeeping Open Questions
meetings.summary: Design session with DP and Mark that resolved the core ledger mechanics ahead of productionising.
decisions: VAT corrections booked in the next period; <€1,000 folds in without a supplemental return; 3 VAT balance-sheet lines (receivable, payable, corrections/suspense)
Closed periods lock; approval-gated insert-only changes with full audit log
Multi-currency: store FX amount + date rate, month-end revaluation to an unrealized FX gains/losses account
open_questions: DP to compile full reporting requirements list

meetings.title: Next Steps AGL with Mark
meetings.summary: Walked the double-entry GL design toward productionising for Ocean Ionics and the Q3 VAT submission.
decisions: Q1/Q2 Exact close as Q3 opening balance
open_questions: Edit permissions for humans vs agents
next_steps: Reproduce balance check in production by 25 Jun

ce07f00e-b392-40bd-85fb-2a4a831c9c80.title: Make neno the source of truth for all bookkeeping

notes.text: Feature set filed as Linear projects (Backlog) with DP-sourced requirements; requirements are updated on each project's summary directly for now, with the source noted.

ce07f00e-b392-40bd-85fb-2a4a831c9c80.exec_summary: The initiative is now filled out as a backlog: the full feature set has been filed as Linear projects (all Backlog), each with a summary and — where the 23 Jun DP session gave signal — initial requirements. End-to-end AR reconciliation is the one project further along (Planned, David). The 23 Jun DP session confirmed aged-receivables reporting (30/60/90/120+ buckets by customer) as a minimum requirement, captured on a new Financial reporting project; DP is compiling a full reporting requirements list (100+ items) that will frame the scope.
whats_changed: Filed the feature set as Linear projects (Backlog) attached to the initiative, with DP-sourced requirements where available.
23 Jun DP session confirmed aged-receivables analysis (30/60/90/120+ buckets by customer) as a minimum reporting requirement.
DP is compiling a full reporting requirements list (potentially 100+ items) to frame future design.

risks.text: Scope is broad and unsequenced; nothing is staffed yet beyond AR reconciliation being earmarked for David.
Full reporting requirements are not yet enumerated — scope could grow materially once DP's list lands.

decisions.text: Aged-receivables analysis (by customer, 30/60/90/120+ day buckets) is a minimum AR reporting requirement.

Full reporting requirements list still being compiled by DP.
Feature sequencing and ownership for the post-GL bookkeeping scope is not yet decided.
